steps.title: Set up the profit equation
steps.content: Using the profit formula Profit = Revenue - Total Expenses, we can express the situation algebraically. The garden earns 18 dollars per blanket and has 162 dollars in expenses. With 504 dollars in profit:$$504 = 18x - 162$$where $x$ is the number of blankets rented.

steps.title: Solve for the number of blankets
steps.content: Add 162 to both sides:$$666 = 18x$$Divide both sides by 18:$$x = 37$$That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
